questions.text: What was your very first paid job, chore-for-money or way you earned anything as a child/teen?
How old were you and what did a typical day or shift look like in that first job?
What was the first thing you bought with money you earned yourself — how did it feel?
Who was your first boss, supervisor or person who paid you — what do you remember about them?
Did you have a summer job, paper round, babysitting, lawn mowing or other early work experience?
What uniform, tool, badge, apron or piece of equipment from your early jobs stands out in your memory?
What was the most surprising or unexpected thing you learned about work when you first started?
Who was a coworker, mentor or older colleague in your early jobs that helped you or made it more bearable?
Was there a small win, compliment or accomplishment at work early on that made you feel proud?
Did you ever have a funny, embarrassing or chaotic moment at your first job?
Looking back, what skill, habit or mindset did you pick up in your early working years that you still use?
Was there a time in your early jobs when you felt overwhelmed, underpaid, undervalued or out of your depth?
Which early job, boss or coworker taught you the most important lesson about work or yourself?
Did you ever quit, get let go from or walk away from an early job — how did that feel at the time?
What part of your early work life do you now realise was more about discovering who you were than about money?
Was there an early career choice or job path you took that you now see as a quiet turning point?
What do you wish you had known or believed about work when you were just starting out?
If you could go back to your first day at your very first job, what advice would you give yourself?
Looking back, what one early work experience still makes you smile, cringe or feel proud today?
What lesson from your early career turned out to be surprisingly important later in life?
Is there an early boss, coworker or job you now wish you could thank or apologise to?
What do you hope your own children or future generations understand about work from your early experiences?
